# abstract-audit

> Solidity contract and project scanner for Abstract (ZKsync L2). Catches EVM incompatibilities before deployment. Zero network requests, built-in rules, instant results.

## Usage

Install: `npx abstract-audit`

Scan current project: `npx abstract-audit`
JSON output for CI: `npx abstract-audit --json`
Filter by severity: `npx abstract-audit --severity high`

## What it catches

### Solidity Source Issues
- selfdestruct, callcode, extcodecopy, pc() (compile-time errors on ZKsync)
- .transfer() and .send() (2300 gas stipend breaks on Abstract, all accounts are smart contracts)
- ecrecover without EIP-1271 support (native account abstraction means not all signers use ECDSA)
- block.coinbase (returns bootloader 0x8001), block.difficulty/prevrandao (returns constant)
- gasleft() without pubdata cost accounting
- extcodesize / tx.origin == msg.sender for EOA checks (unreliable, all accounts are contracts)
- CREATE/CREATE2 in assembly (address derivation differs)

### Config Issues
- Standard solc instead of zksolc
- Hardhat without @matterlabs/hardhat-zksync plugin
- Deprecated individual @matterlabs/hardhat-zksync-* packages

## Programmatic API

```typescript
import { scan, formatJson } from "abstract-audit";
const result = scan("./path/to/project");
console.log(formatJson(result));
```

## Related

- [x402-fetch](https://github.com/LoserLab/x402-fetch): HTTP client for x402 paid API endpoints on Abstract
